insight: OpenMythos analysis: zero code is transferable to agentic-workflows hub (different domains: neural architecture vs knowledge base). Meta-pattern validation confirms our design is sound — recurrent-depth, input injection, ACT halting patterns already present in our phase system.

Codebase understanding optimal stack: Tree-sitter repo map + BM25 retrieval + bash-only agentic exploration + large context with prompt caching. Trend is toward simpler agent interfaces as models get smarter (mini-swe-agent proves 100-line bash-only agent beats complex tool interfaces on SWE-bench).
Reflection loop works best when called after commit, not before
The pipefail + grep pattern causes double-counting. Fix: use grep -c with || true instead of wc -l with trailing or
Error handling: forgot to add set -e to new script, wasted 10min debugging

Ruflo v3.7.0-alpha.26 evaluated: CLI works (26 commands), MCP server (266 tools at 168MB RSS), daemon (5 workers at 146MB RSS), hooks pretrain (1107 files). Memory init hangs on WSL2 (sql.js SIGBUS) --- use agentmemory instead. Tier 1 integrated: MCP server to opencode.jsonc, hooks post-task to task-retrospect, availability to session-start. Decision: auto-learning + MCP tools = DO, daemon = conditional TRY, GOAP/plugins/vector DB = SKIP.

Slice 2 complete: ruflo daemon running under systemd (ruflo-daemon.service) at 74.5MB RSS. 5 workers active (map, audit, optimize, consolidate, testgaps) with 2-30min intervals. Zero overlap with detect-gaps.sh. Audit worker scans env protection, gitignore, secrets. systemd auto-restart verified. loginctl enable-linger set for boot survival.

Slice 3 complete: ruflo plugin marketplace evaluated --- 20 plugins via IPFS/Pinata, 7s+ fetch latency. Plugins are TypeScript npm packages (src/index.ts, hooks.ts) --- fundamentally different from our markdown skills with companion scripts. Plugin create command appears to have a scaffolding bug (no files written). Conclusion: propagate-to-all.sh is superior for our skill distribution --- instant, local, no rewrite, no IPFS dependency. SKIP plugin bridge.

Ruflo integration finalized: CLI-only model. MCP server disabled (saved 168MB/session), daemon stopped + systemd disabled (saved 74.5MB always-on). All ruflo value via CLI commands: hooks post-task in task-retrospect.sh, hooks pretrain/route/session-restore available on demand. Zero persistent cost vs 242MB before. Clean integration: no daemon polling in session-start.sh, just CLI hints.

teambrilliant/tap-skills and dev-skills are high-quality low-star repos with genuinely unique skills --- 9 of 17 had no overlap with our 27 skills. Signature blocks + enhanced frontmatter (trigger-phrases, handoffs) add significant value with minimal code change.
Sub-agent patterns (fan-out, thin-result, pre-flight->execute, fail-escalate) documented in docs/workflow.md --- adopted from teambrilliant/dev-skills context-efficiency patterns.

Macro-to-Micro Funnel --- researched systematic debugging methodologies (top-down design, Wolf fence, delta debugging, Cynefin) and integrated as default fix conduct across 3 files. Now automatic: every fix starts at system architecture, drills to root cause. No need to say 'start from architectural/systemic/macro to micro' anymore.

Built the Agent Research Methodology --- a 6-phase (Frame -> Discover Local -> Gather External -> Triangulate -> Apply -> Preserve) general-purpose research engine for agents. Replaced AI-topic-specific research prompt. Added architecture analysis specialization with current-state mapping, best-practice research, gap analysis, and transformation planning. Integrated with macro-to-micro funnel so Level 1 can now research architecture beyond just fixing. This is now the automatic default for any research task.

Cognitive surrender resistance integrated across 7 workflow files (AGENTS.md, docs/workflow.md, 3 commands, 2 skills). 3 core heuristics: construct expectation before output, calibration check after output, ask model to argue against itself for tradeoffs. Full evidence synthesis in research/cognitive-surrender-research.md (400+ lines, 21 sources). Key learning: websearch vs webfetch is a critical capability difference --- URL-guessing systematically misses top-ranking content.

RTK (Rust Token Killer) integrates into agentic-workflows at 3 tiers: Tier 1 --- zero-code install (curl install + rtk init -g --opencode); Tier 2 --- patch 31 lines across 5 files (session-start, context-pressure, detect-gaps, task-retrospect, workflow.md) to surface RTK analytics; Tier 3 --- full workflow loop integration. 78 scripts + 8 hooks + 14 commands + 3 subagent types all benefit from transparent bash compression. OpenCode plugin is the correct integration path.

12-factor-agents integration: our ~80% implicit alignment means the integration is primarily about documentation + filling the 3 gaps (human-in-the-loop, control flow interrupts, error counter). The A2H protocol draft and workshop walkthrough are gold mines for the next slices.

Slice 2: XML-style context output (--xml flag), deterministic pre-fetch (scripts/prefetch-context.sh), and events array in session-state.json. Key insight: the same pre-fetch pattern works as both a standalone script and embedded in retrieve-context.sh. XML format is significantly more compact than JSON for LLM consumption.

Slice 3: A2H protocol implementation (scripts/a2h-contact.sh with contact/approve/respond/list), multi-channel notification dispatcher (scripts/notify.sh with Slack/CLI/file), approval gate in implement-preflight.sh (--risk high), and human-checkpoint command in pipeline-run.sh. Key insight: the most valuable A2H pattern is the approve/respond flow for high-stakes operations --- the CLI-interactive mode creates a real interrupt-between-selection-and-execution gate.

Slice 4: create-hl-agent.sh scaffold script and propagation template. The scaffold creates a complete 12-factor agent project with AGENTS.md, session-state.json with events array, and copies of all scripts (a2h-contact, notify, prefetch-context, checkpoint). The propagation template follows the existing .template.sh pattern and can be synced to topic folders via the propagation system.

Slice 5: error-counter.sh with increment/check/reset/context/list. Auto-escalates to human via A2H after N consecutive failures. Combined with log-error.sh (capture) and a2h-contact.sh (escalate) forms a complete therapeutic pipeline: capture -> count -> context -> escalate. All 5 integration milestones now complete.

RTK integration critical finding: OpenCode plugin mutates args.command in tool.execute.before, which means RTK-rewritten commands bypass bash permission deny rules. Fix requires dual deny rules (e.g., 'git commit *': 'deny' AND 'rtk git commit *': 'deny') in opencode.jsonc. Also, RTK only intercepts agent-issued Bash tool calls, NOT internal subprocesses --- all 78 workspace scripts run internal commands directly and are unaffected. Script safety is total.
